signals.headline: Series Outlook**: The Raptors are facing a potential 9-year playoff drought if they lose this series[6]
signals.raw:
Series Outlook**: The Raptors are facing a potential 9-year playoff drought if they lose this series[6]. Games 3 and 4 are scheduled for April 23 and April 26, respectively, both at Scotiabank Arena in Toronto[1].

No major roster moves, injuries, or rule changes have been announced that would alter market predictions since Game 2.

signals.headline: Raptors' Offensive Struggles**: Brandon Ingram has been a critical weakness, scoring just 7 points on 3-15 shooting in G
signals.raw: Raptors' Offensive Struggles**: Brandon Ingram has been a critical weakness, scoring just 7 points on 3-15 shooting in Game 2, while the team overall has struggled with turnovers and point production[1][6]. RJ Barrett has been the bright spot with 24 points in Game 1 and strong performances continuing[1].

signals.headline: Toronto Raptors Trail Cleveland Cavaliers 0-2 in First-Round Series | NO (Series Not Decided) | HIGH Confidence**
signals.raw:
Toronto Raptors Trail Cleveland Cavaliers 0-2 in First-Round Series | NO (Series Not Decided) | HIGH Confidence**

The Raptors face elimination in their first-round matchup against the 4th-seeded Cavaliers, with the series now shifting to Toronto for Game 3 on Thursday, April 23 at 8:00 pm ET[1]. Cleveland has decisively won both games: 126-113 in Game 1 (Saturday)[1] and 115-105 in Game 2 (Monday
matched_markets: NBA Playoffs: Who Will Win Series? - Raptors vs. Cavaliers

signals.headline: Cavaliers' Dominant Backcourt**: Donovan Mitchell scored 32 points in Game 1 and 30 in Game 2, while James Harden added
signals.raw: Cavaliers' Dominant Backcourt**: Donovan Mitchell scored 32 points in Game 1 and 30 in Game 2, while James Harden added 28 points in Game 2, establishing a commanding presence that the Raptors have struggled to counter[1]. This marks only the 4th trio in Cavaliers playoff history to score 25+ points each in a single game[6].
